method: TERMINAL TOTAL-SLOT: inscription-FINAL word-form over-representation. Candidate = word-form with most inscription-FINAL occurrences among forms with total>=8 AND final>=5 (frozen selection rule, by final count). Metric = candidate final-position enrichment (final occurrences / all occurrences in >=2-word inscriptions). Null = within-inscription word-order position permutation (>=2000 draws, one-sided). LB positive control (DAMOS, seeded pseudo-inscriptions, no site metadata) gates the verdict: DETECT planted terminal bias + FALSE-POSITIVE on position-randomized sets.
result: Global candidate (anonymous single-sign sequence 'NI') shows significant inscription-FINAL enrichment (final_enrichment=0.310, p=0.017, initial_rate=0.085 control). However the effect is NOT robust across sites: only 2 sites have >=10 candidate occurrences and NEITHER is individually significant (Haghia Triada p=0.055, Khania p=0.266); the aggregate final-slot concentration fallback yields only 1/5 sites significant (Haghia Triada alone); and leave-one-site-out excluding Haghia Triada collapses the global signal (p=0.132). The terminal-slot structure is concentrated in / driven by the largest site.
key_findings: The frozen selection rule surfaced a single anonymous sign sequence ('NI') as the most inscription-FINAL word-form meeting the threshold (final=22, total=71); it is treated purely positionally with no phonetic/semantic reading.

GLOBALLY the candidate shows significant inscription-FINAL enrichment: final_enrichment=0.310 vs position-permutation null_mean final-count 14.69, one-sided p=0.017; its initial-position rate is only 0.085 (control), consistent with a FINAL-slot, not initial-slot, role.

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED: the machinery DETECTS a planted terminal bias on LB (p=0.0, correct direction) and has a FALSE-POSITIVE rate of 0.067 (<=0.10) on position-randomized pseudo-inscriptions, so the global signal is not a machinery artifact.

The effect is NOT cross-site robust: only 2 sites have >=10 candidate occurrences and NEITHER is individually significant (Haghia Triada p=0.055, Khania p=0.266); the aggregate final-slot concentration fallback yields only 1/5 sites significant (Haghia Triada alone).

Leave-one-site-out excluding the largest site (Haghia Triada) COLLAPSES the global signal (p=0.132), confirming the terminal-slot structure is concentrated in / driven by Haghia Triada rather than being a pan-Cretan document-structural regularity.
successor_hypotheses: E036: Test whether the 'NI' terminal-slot signal at Haghia Triada is itself document-internal (e.g. line-final / section-final within multi-line tablets) vs inscription-final only, using the stream's nl/div tokens as sub-document position markers.

E037: Replicate the terminal-slot test on a Haghia-Triada-EXCLUDED corpus to see whether ANY other form carries a cross-site terminal signal, or whether the terminal-slot role is genuinely HT-specific.

E038: Pair the E034 A-initial heading signal with this terminal signal: do inscriptions tend to OPEN with an A-initial word AND CLOSE with the terminal form (a bracketing/heading+total document frame)?

E039: Test whether the terminal-slot concentration correlates with inscription LENGTH (short ledger-like inscriptions vs long narrative tablets) to distinguish a 'total/summary' structural role from a generic end-marker.

E040: Apply the same frozen candidate-selection rule to the Linear B positive-control corpus (with real inscription boundaries where available) to check whether a terminal-slot form exists there and whether it is cross-site robust, calibrating the SITE_LOCAL vs CROSS_SITE_ROBUST distinction.
non_circular: Word-forms are ANONYMOUS sign sequences carrying no phonetic/sound/meaning/reading. The candidate was selected by a FROZEN rule (most inscription-final occurrences among forms with total>=8 AND final>=5), committed in the prereg BEFORE any p-value was computed; the surfaced identifier 'NI' is reported only as an anonymous sign sequence and the verdict does not depend on any assumed value of it. All statistics are L2/L3 positional (within-inscription word-order permutation). LB is a positive-control benchmark ONLY (DAMOS has no site metadata -> seeded pseudo-inscription partition, stated explicitly); it certifies the machinery detects a planted terminal bias and does not fire on noise, and is never used to read or validate a Linear A claim.
deviations: The candidate 'NI' has >=10 occurrences in only 2 sites, so per the protocol the aggregate final-slot CONCENTRATION fallback metric was ALSO run (>=15 qualifying inscriptions per site); both metrics are reported. The verdict uses the candidate metric as primary (metric_used='candidate') with the aggregate as supporting evidence; both agree the effect is not cross-site robust.
No other deviations from the frozen protocol.
